slides.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 1; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
slides.narration_text: Every clinician recognizes the tension in the approved clip: a busy hospital corridor, then a physician alone with a dense electronic health record. That motion turns the slide's three labels - The Training Gap, The Mindset Shift, and The Opportunity - into a real problem statement. We were trained to follow evidence-based pathways, but not taught how to redesign them. The innovation mindset begins when you stop seeing yourself as only a reactor inside the system and start acting as a designer of the next pathway, with enough structure and psychological safety to pursue change.

matched_visual_references.element: three bottom callouts: The Training Gap, The Mindset Shift, The Opportunity
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: The Training Gap, The Mindset Shift, and The Opportunity

slides.script_notes: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 1; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.

slides.visual_description: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 2; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: Under the title An Innovation Mindset in Healthcare, the central definition does the real work. An innovation mindset is the internal conviction that you are not just an employee within a healthcare system, but a designer of it. That sentence reframes clinical expertise as a bridge between current limitations and future possibilities. The point is not to abandon evidence-based practice. It is to use the same rigor, empathy, and pattern recognition you already bring to patient care to redesign the systems that keep generating avoidable friction.

matched_visual_references.element: centered definition statement about being a designer of the healthcare system
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: the central definition

slides.script_notes: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 2; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

slides.visual_description: literal-visual slide generated by Gamma for card 3; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: On this roadmap, Stage 1, Stage 2, and Stage 3 mark the full arc of the certificate, not three disconnected topics. Stage 1 is Sparking Innovation: Insight to Opportunity. Stage 2 is Building the Solution: Design to Pitch. Stage 3 is Driving Impact: Project to Change. The slide makes clear that Course 1 teaches you to see your daily clinical world differently before you are asked to build or scale anything. In other words, this first module is about learning to distinguish a recurring frustration from a real opportunity worth developing.

matched_visual_references.element: three-stage roadmap labeled Sparking Innovation, Building the Solution, Driving Impact
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Stage 1, Stage 2, and Stage 3

slides.script_notes: literal-visual slide generated by Gamma for card 3; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

slides.visual_description: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 4; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: The objective list on the slide starts with Define and Correlate and ends with Create, and that ordering matters. This module first asks you to define the innovation mindset, then analyze the structural trends that make physician leadership necessary, then differentiate an idea from an opportunity, then evaluate failures through root-cause analysis, and finally create an innovation baseline and opportunity radar. The slide is not just a checklist. It is a scaffold from understanding to action, with Bloom's levels showing that the course expects progressively more judgment, not just recall.

matched_visual_references.element: stacked objective rows progressing from Define and Correlate to Create
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Define and Correlate and ends with Create

slides.script_notes: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 4; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

slides.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 5; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
slides.narration_text: Across the three innovator profiles - Thomas Fogarty, Peter Pronovost, and Patricia Bath - the slide argues that physicians have always shaped the future of medicine by staying close to the problem. Fogarty's catheter changed vascular surgery, Pronovost's checklist transformed patient safety, and Bath's Laserphaco Probe advanced cataract treatment. Those examples matter because they span device, process, and technological innovation. The lesson is that clinical proximity is not a limitation. It is often the starting advantage that lets a physician see a solvable system failure before anyone else does.

matched_visual_references.element: three innovator profiles: Thomas Fogarty, Peter Pronovost, Patricia Bath
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Thomas Fogarty, Peter Pronovost, and Patricia Bath

slides.script_notes: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 5; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.

slides.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 6; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
slides.narration_text: In the left panel, The Foundation is explicit: innovation cannot flourish in fear. On the right, Why It Matters tells you why psychological safety is operational, not decorative. If people cannot name what is broken, they cannot redesign what comes next. The callout that half-baked ideas have value matters here because early innovation work is supposed to be unfinished. A team that punishes vulnerability shuts down the very observations that reveal system flaws. Growth mindset and psychological safety are therefore prerequisites for honest diagnosis, shared learning, and intelligent failure.

matched_visual_references.element: callout that half-baked ideas have value
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: half-baked ideas have value

slides.script_notes: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 6; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.

slides.visual_description: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 7; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: On this slide, the objective statement and the Bloom's Taxonomy Level sit side by side to define the M&M of Innovation. The learner is asked to evaluate systemic operational failures, conduct root-cause analyses, and defend findings in a peer-reviewed forum. That pairing matters because evaluation is not passive agreement. It requires judgment, justification, and evidence. The assignment format borrows the discipline of clinical morbidity and mortality review, but redirects it toward operational design problems, where the goal is not blame but a defensible account of why a failure keeps recurring.

matched_visual_references.element: side-by-side objective statement and Bloom's Taxonomy Level panel
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: the objective statement and the Bloom's Taxonomy Level sit side by side

slides.script_notes: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 7; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

slides.visual_description: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 8; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: The slide splits the work into Personal Innovation Baseline and Opportunity Radar so the learner sees both the self-assessment and the practice-based scanning task. First, you establish a baseline for your own innovation capacity. Then you capture real-world clinical frictions worth revisiting later as vetted opportunities. Bloom's Level: Create is important here because the course is asking for original artifacts, not just reflection. By the end of this module, you should have a structured way to notice recurring breakdowns instead of letting them disappear into the routine of the workday.

matched_visual_references.element: split headings Personal Innovation Baseline and Opportunity Radar
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Personal Innovation Baseline and Opportunity Radar

slides.script_notes: literal-text slide generated by Gamma for card 8; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.

slides.visual_description: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 9; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.
slides.narration_text: The three numbered prompts - Introduce Yourself, Name Your Bias or Assumption, and Reframe as an Opportunity - turn reflection into a method. You begin by naming your role and one recurring frustration. Then you surface the story you have been telling yourself about why the problem persists. Finally, you reframe that frustration as something that can be examined and redesigned. That sequence matters because innovation rarely starts with a polished solution. It starts by making hidden assumptions visible enough to challenge them.

matched_visual_references.element: three numbered prompts: Introduce Yourself, Name Your Bias or Assumption, Reframe as an Opportunity
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: Introduce Yourself, Name Your Bias or Assumption, and Reframe as an Opportunity

slides.script_notes: Creative slide generated by Gamma for card 9; final visual grounding occurs in perception artifacts.

slides.visual_description: literal-visual slide generated by Gamma for card 10; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
slides.narration_text: On the roadmap, the You Are Here marker at Module 1 places this lesson inside the larger APC pathway. Course 1 builds the foundations of innovation leadership, Course 2 moves into tools for an innovation leader, and Course 3 shifts to establishing viability through execution. This slide is contextual by design. It reminds you that Module 1 is the opening step in a longer progression from insight to opportunity, from design to pitch, and finally from project to lasting change. The immediate task is not to master the whole roadmap today. It is to understand where this module sits and what it prepares you to do next.

matched_visual_references.element: roadmap with You Are Here marker at Module 1 across three courses
matched_visual_references.narration_cue: the You Are Here marker at Module 1

slides.script_notes: literal-visual slide generated by Gamma for card 10; source text constraints enforced for literal fidelity.
